rationale: Earth & Environmental at L3 has the cleanest physical-system mapping of any L1 — atmosphere, oceans, ice, water, soil, rock, biosphere. Thirty-six leaves split inside each subsystem by what's being studied (e.g. atmospheric science = dynamics + aerosols + cloud physics + boundary layer). Climate science gets 4 leaves because climate-model-development, paleoclimate, attribution, and climate-impacts are distinct working communities.

subfields.name: Climate Modeling & CMIP-Class Simulations
subfields.tagline: GCM development, CMIP analysis, regional climate downscaling.

subfields.name: Paleoclimate & Proxy Reconstruction
subfields.tagline: Paleoclimate proxies, Holocene/last-deglaciation, ice-core records.

subfields.name: Climate Attribution & Sensitivity
subfields.tagline: Detection-attribution, climate sensitivity estimation, feedback analysis.

subfields.name: Climate Extremes & Impacts
subfields.tagline: Heatwaves, drought, climate-impact projections, regional impacts.

subfields.name: Weather Prediction & Atmospheric Dynamics
subfields.tagline: NWP, dynamics of weather systems, jet streams, blocking events.

subfields.name: Aerosols, Clouds & Boundary Layer
subfields.tagline: Aerosol-cloud interactions, cloud microphysics, ABL dynamics.

subfields.name: Atmospheric Chemistry Transport
subfields.tagline: Atmospheric chemistry models, transport of pollutants, ozone chemistry.

subfields.name: Physical Oceanography & Circulation
subfields.tagline: Ocean circulation, AMOC, currents, ocean mixing, mesoscale eddies.

subfields.name: Ocean Biogeochemistry
subfields.tagline: Carbon and nutrient cycling in oceans, ocean acidification chemistry.

subfields.name: Sea Ice & Cryospheric Oceanography
subfields.tagline: Sea ice dynamics, ocean-ice interactions, Arctic/Antarctic ocean systems.

subfields.name: Coastal & Shelf Oceanography
subfields.tagline: Shelf systems, estuarine processes, coastal upwelling, ocean dead zones.

subfields.name: Watershed & Catchment Hydrology
subfields.tagline: Surface hydrology, catchment processes, runoff modeling.

subfields.name: Groundwater & Subsurface Hydrology
subfields.tagline: Aquifer dynamics, groundwater modeling, subsurface flow.

subfields.name: Snow & Glacier Hydrology
subfields.tagline: Snow hydrology, glacier melt, mountain hydrology.

subfields.name: Drought & Flood Hydrology
subfields.tagline: Drought science, flood modeling, hydrological extremes.

subfields.name: Structural Geology & Tectonics
subfields.tagline: Tectonics, structural geology, orogeny, continental dynamics.

subfields.name: Igneous & Metamorphic Petrology
subfields.tagline: Igneous petrology, metamorphic petrology, melt dynamics.

subfields.name: Sedimentology & Stratigraphy
subfields.tagline: Sedimentary systems, sequence stratigraphy, basin analysis.

subfields.name: Geomorphology & Surface Processes
subfields.tagline: Landscape evolution, surface processes, sediment transport, erosion.

subfields.name: Seismology & Earthquake Source
subfields.tagline: Earthquake source physics, seismicity, fault mechanics, slow slip.

subfields.name: Deep Earth & Tomography
subfields.tagline: Mantle/core structure, seismic tomography, deep Earth dynamics.

subfields.name: Geomagnetism & Paleomagnetism
subfields.tagline: Geomagnetism, paleomagnetism, geodynamo, magnetic anomalies.

subfields.name: Isotope Geochemistry & Geochronology
subfields.tagline: Stable/radiogenic isotopes, geochronology, isotope provenance.

subfields.name: Low-T Geochemistry & Biogeochemistry
subfields.tagline: Low-temperature aqueous geochemistry, biogeochemical cycles, geobiology.

subfields.name: Carbon Cycle & Ecosystem Flux
subfields.tagline: Carbon cycle, eddy-covariance flux, soil carbon, ocean carbon uptake.

subfields.name: Nitrogen & Nutrient Cycling
subfields.tagline: Nitrogen cycling, N deposition, nutrient cycling in ecosystems.

subfields.name: Terrestrial Ecosystem & Land Models
subfields.tagline: Terrestrial biosphere models, vegetation dynamics, land carbon-climate feedbacks.

subfields.name: Satellite & Multispectral Remote Sensing
subfields.tagline: Multispectral satellite RS, Landsat/Sentinel applications, LULC mapping.

subfields.name: SAR & Microwave Remote Sensing
subfields.tagline: Synthetic aperture radar, InSAR for surface change, soil moisture microwave.

subfields.name: LiDAR & Hyperspectral Remote Sensing
subfields.tagline: LiDAR for terrain/forest structure, hyperspectral analysis.

subfields.name: ML for Earth Observation
subfields.tagline: Foundation models for EO, segmentation for satellite imagery, geo-ML benchmarks.

subfields.name: Wastewater Treatment Engineering
subfields.tagline: Wastewater treatment processes, MBR, advanced oxidation, nutrient recovery.

subfields.name: Air Pollution Control & Indoor Air
subfields.tagline: Emission control, indoor air quality, particulate matter engineering.

subfields.name: Soil & Water Contamination Remediation
subfields.tagline: Soil remediation, groundwater remediation, PFAS removal.

subfields.name: Energy Systems & Decarbonization Pathways
subfields.tagline: Energy-system modeling, decarbonization pathways, transition scenarios.

subfields.name: Urban Sustainability & Industrial Ecology
subfields.tagline: Urban metabolism, LCA, industrial ecology, sustainable cities.

subfields.name: Seismology & Earthquake Hazards
subfields.tagline: Earthquake source physics, seismic hazard, induced seismicity, early warning.

subfields.name: Volcanology, Landslides & Ground-Failure Risk
subfields.tagline: Eruption forecasting, volcanic plumes, landslide triggers, multi-hazard risk.

subfields.name: Glaciology & Ice-Sheet Science
subfields.tagline: Ice-sheet mass balance, sea-level contribution, glacier hydrology — ice cores, radar, altimetry, ice-flow modeling.

subfields.name: Permafrost, Snow & Sea Ice
subfields.tagline: Permafrost thaw, snow cover, sea-ice dynamics, polar feedbacks.

uncovered_concerns: Natural Hazards & Disaster Science L2 has its papers folding under earthquake source, geomorphology (landslides), and climate extremes leaves depending on the angle.
Polar & Cryospheric Science L2 papers fold across sea ice, snow/glacier hydrology, and deep-time paleobiology depending on the lens.
